title: Classical Ciphers and Cryptanalysis as Defect Labs · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Xor Reuse Break
summary: A training tool presents Caesar, substitution, repeating-key XOR, and one-time pads as equivalent encryption choices and accidentally ships the toy implementation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the xor reuse break increment in classical cipher attack and migration dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.
Implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Xor Reuse Break · Theory Crypto Go Xor Reuse Break
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7338 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Xor Reuse Break contract for the supplied data.
